text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill. First, practice good time management by breaking large projects into smaller tasks and setting personal mini-deadlines. Prioritization is also key—identify which tasks are most urgent or important and tackle those first. Next, manage the scope of your work: be realistic about what you can achieve within the given time, and learn to say no to extra requests that could delay you. Finally, communicate openly. If you anticipate a problem, let your team or manager know early. Asking for help or negotiating a revised deadline is better than delivering late work.
